title: T0: Mirror Void
description: A slow-burn meta-horror mod that induces paranoia
body:
**T0: Mirror Void**

T0: Mirror Void is a meta-horror experience for Minecraft that blurs the line between the game and your reality. This mod isn't about jump-scares; it's about the slow, agonizing realization that you are no longer the master of your own world.

**🌑 The Entity: T0**
Something has entered your code. T0 is a persistent observer that tracks your progress, studies your habits, and slowly begins to overwrite the game’s logic. You might see him in the corner of your eye, or find "gifts" he left behind.

**🏠 The Mimicry (Dynamic World Horror)****
T0 is obsessed with your creations. Using a unique real-time scanning system, the mod can clone your own buildings. Imagine wandering far from home, only to find an exact, corrupted replica of your base standing in the middle of nowhere. A house that looks like yours, but feels... wrong.

**🔊 Atmospheric Immersion**
Original Russian Voice Acting: To maintain the authentic and eerie atmosphere of the original vision, all entity voices and scripted sequences feature high-quality Russian voice acting. Even if you don't speak the language, the cold, distorted tone of T0 will let you know exactly what he thinks of you.

**Audio Anomalies: Distorted sounds, whispers in your house, and "system" glitches that react to your actions.**

**⚡ The Meta-Final (The Ritual)**
Everything leads to the Mirror Void. Once the ritual begins, there is no turning back.

**Identity Overwrite: T0 doesn't want to kill you; he wants to be you.**

**Unique Ending:** The ending can be enabled by renaming any item to T0. However, I only recommend doing this if you're tired of the mod or for other reasons. The mod features a scripted finale that interacts with your operating system, mimicking a critical system failure. When the screen fades and the "errors" pop up, remember: The user change is complete.

**⚠️ Warning**
This mod is designed to be unpredictable. It may simulate system crashes, manipulate your camera, and "lock" your world in the final act.
